content.full_text:
90-347
SB

ERICKSON
ENTERPRISES

Redevelopment

June 29, 1990

Ms Katherine Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Sixth Floor
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Re: James E. Erickson's Request for Informal Opinion

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Planning

John Shaw, San Juan Capistrano's City Attorney, has informed me that you
requested additional information as to the legal ownership of my consulting
business, Erickson Enterprises. The business is a sole proprietorship doing
business as Erickson Enterprises. The income received by the business as well as
that which I personally derive from it is my sole and separate property. My
husband and I set the business up in this fashion in order to encourage an
independent professional career for me.

Enclosed for you information is the Fictitious Business Name Statement printed in
the local newspaper and filed with the Orange County Clerk. Also enclosed is the
agreement between my husband and myself formally agreeing that all income from
Erickson Enterprises is my sole and separate property.

I hope the information provided adequately answers your questions. If you have
any additional questions as to the organizational structure of my business or the
nature of my work for the San Juan Capistrano Community Redevelopment Agency
please do no hesitate to contact me at (714) 496-7576.

Project
Management

Very truly yours,

Nancy L Erickson

31762
Paseo Terraza
San Juan
Capistrano
CA
92675

(714) 496-7576
FAX: 496-8919

AFFIDAVIT OF PUBLICATION

This space is for the County Clerk's Filing Stamp

ORIGINAL FILED
COUNTY CLERK
ORANGE COUNTY

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

County of Orange

I am a citizen of the United States and a resident of the
County aforesaid; I am over the age of twenty one years
and not a party to or interested in the above-entitled
matter. I am the principal clerk of the Capistrano Val-
ley News, a newspaper of general circulation in the City
of San Juan Capistrano, County of Orange, and which
newspaper has been adjudicated to be a newspaper of
general circulation by the Superior Court of the County
of Orange, State of California, June 5, 1984, Case No.
A-122949 in and for the City of San Juan Capistrano,
County of Orange and the South Orange County Judicial
District; that the notice, of which the annexed is a true
printed copy, has been published in each regular and
entire issue of said newspaper and not in any supple-
ment thereof on the following dates, to wit:

June 22,29, July 6,13,1989

I declare under penalty of perjury that the foregoing is
true and correct.

Executed at Mission Viejo, Orange County

California, this .13. . . . day of July . . . 1989 . . . . . .

Proof of Publication of

FICTITIOUS BUSINESS NAME STATEMENT.........

FICTITIOUS
BUSINESS NAME
STATEMENT F422266

The following person(s) are
doing business as:

ERICKSON ENTERPRISES
31762 Peace Terrace
San Juan Capistrano,
Orange County, CA 92675
31762 PEACE TERRACE
San Juan Capistrano,
Orange County, 92675

This business is conducted
by an individual.

The registrant commenced
to transact business under the
fictitious business name or
names listed above on June 19,
1989.

/s/ NANCY LYNN ERICKSON
This statement was filed
with the County Clerk of Or-
ange County on June 14, 1989.
Published: Capistrano Valley
News June 22, 29, July 6, 13,
1989.

[signature]
Signature

Capistrano Valley News
23811 Via Fabricante
P.O. Box 3629
Mission Viejo, Calif., 92690

FILE NUMBER F422266

ERICKSON ENTERPRISES
SEPARATE PROPERTY AGREEMENT

JUNE 3, 1989

1. Nancy and James Erickson are married to each other; and

2. In order to create more flexibility in her schedule, variety in the scope of her work and
greater economic opportunity, Nancy intends to resign her position as Community
Development Administrator for the City of San Juan Capistrano and establish a
consulting business to be known as "Erickson Enterprises"; and

3. In order to encourage Erickson Enterprises to expand and flourish, and provide Nancy
an independent base for her professional career, we agree that all income from the
business may be retained by Nancy as her sole and separate property; and

4. In order further to facilitate the establishment of Erickson Enterprises, Jim agrees to a
loan of community assets in an amount not to exceed $20,000 for purchase of
necessary equipment, supplies, and provision of other necessary working capital; and

5. Jim also offers his full support, best wishes and confidence in the bright future of
Erickson Enterprises.

James E. Erickson

Nancy L. Erickson
